ontology.foodex2_description: Pasta and noodles, uncooked — egg-based noodle product
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A000E covers pasta/noodle category; closest parent match for egg-based noodle product. No exact FoodEx2 code for egg noodles uncooked exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving of uncooked egg noodles is approximately 80g dry weight, which yields roughly 160–180g cooked noodles after boiling.

nutrient_source.title: Egg Noodles, Uncooked — Singapore Health Promotion Board (HPB) Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per-100g nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for 'Egg noodles, uncooked' (cr_id F05070011). Saturated fat not reported by HPB; value estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 20110 (Egg noodles, unenriched, dry) as closest approved secondary source. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported by HPB and set to -1.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 58 for egg noodles is consistent with values reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International GI Tables (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008). Egg noodles (boiled) GI ~57–58 reported in peer-reviewed literature. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (58 × 71.27 × 0.80) / 100 ≈ 33.1, rounded to 33.0 for an 80g dry serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (21 mg/100g) with moderate potassium (244 mg/100g) supporting a favourable Na:K ratio
hypertension.guidance: Uncooked egg noodles are low in sodium and suitable as a grain base for hypertension management; however, sodium content in the final dish will increase substantially with added sauces, broths, or seasonings, so preparation with low-sodium condiments is essential.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate content (71.3g/100g uncooked) and medium GI (58) resulting in a high glycemic load per serving
type2_diabetes.guidance: Egg noodles have a high carbohydrate density on an uncooked basis and a medium GI; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to approximately 40–60g dry weight, pair with high-fibre vegetables and lean protein to blunt postprandial glycaemic response, and note that cooked weight approximately doubles, halving carbohydrate per 100g cooked.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate cholesterol content (84 mg/100g) from egg, low saturated fat (~0.9g/100g), and low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Egg noodles contain moderate dietary cholesterol from egg content; total fat is low at 4.4g/100g with low saturated fat, and sodium is very low, making them acceptable in a heart-healthy diet when consumed in controlled portions and prepared without high-fat or high-sodium sauces.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Egg noodles (uncooked) are a dried wheat-based noodle product made from wheat flour, egg, water, and salt. They are characteristically yellow in colour due to the egg content, with a firm and springy texture when cooked. Egg noodles are widely used across Western and Asian cuisines — appearing in dishes such as pasta, lo mein, chow mein, and Singapore-style noodle dishes. They are prepared by boiling in water for 3–5 minutes until al dente, after which the cooked weight approximately doubles. Per 100g uncooked, egg noodles provide 384 kcal, 14.2g protein, 4.4g fat, and 71.3g carbohydrate with 3.3g dietary fibre. Sodium is very low at 21mg/100g, making them suitable for hypertension management when prepared without high-sodium sauces. The medium glycemic index (GI 58) and high carbohydrate density make portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Cholesterol is moderate at 84mg/100g due to egg content. Egg noodles contain gluten and egg allergens and are not suitable for vegans or those with coeliac disease. They are a good source of protein relative to other noodle types and provide moderate iron (1.9mg/100g) and potassium (244mg/100g).
common_user_queries: How many calories in egg noodles?
Egg noodles vs rice noodles nutrition
How much protein in egg noodles?
Are egg noodles suitable for hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database (cr_id F05070011, confidence 0.95) with exact match. GI value of 58 is consistent with University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature for egg noodles. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central as closest approved secondary source and documented. All mandatory fields populated.
notes: Saturated fat (0.9g/100g) estimated from USDA FoodData Central NDB 20110 (Egg noodles, unenriched, dry) as HPB does not report this field; this is an adapted value from an approved secondary source. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: these values are not reported in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database for this entry, and detailed fatty acid fractions, B-vitamin panel, and moisture data are not available in the HPB source as of 2026. USDA FoodData Central (NDB 20110) was consulted for secondary confirmation of macros and saturated fat only. All per-100g values are on an uncooked/dry-weight basis; cooked weight approximately doubles, halving nutrient density per 100g cooked. Glycemic load per serving calculated for an 80g dry serving: (58 × 71.27 × 0.80) / 100 ≈ 33.1, rounded to 33.0. The food is classified as 'western' in the input cultural field; however, egg noodles are widely used across Asian cuisines including Chinese, Singaporean, and Southeast Asian contexts — multilingual names have been provided accordingly. Halal status is not confirmed as egg noodles may contain non-halal egg processing; marked false pending certification verification.
